user_query:
Build a small curated catalog of community skills using the dataset provided. Read both input/request.json and input/skills.jsonl. Apply the following rules precisely:

1) Filtering
- Only consider skills whose category is in request.categories.
- Require downloads >= request.min_downloads.
- Require updated_at >= request.min_updated_at (treat updated_at as an ISO8601 timestamp; compare lexically as UTC strings or parse to dates).
- Also require that each kept skill matches at least one keyword from request.keywords. A skill "matches" a keyword if the lowercase keyword appears as a substring (case-insensitive) in any of the following fields: name, description, or any tag.

2) Sorting and selection
- Group remaining skills by category.
- Within each category, sort by downloads (descending). Break ties by stars (descending), then by updated_at (descending, ISO order).
- Take the top request.top_n_per_category items from each category after sorting.

3) Write output/shortlist.csv with exactly this header and column order:
category,rank,slug,name,downloads,stars,updated_at,matched_keywords,tags
- rank is 1..N within each category according to the sorted order.
- matched_keywords is a comma-separated list (no spaces) of the keywords from request.keywords that appear in that skill (based on the matching rule above), listed in the same order they appear in request.keywords. If none match, leave it blank.
- tags is the skill's tags joined by semicolons in the order provided in the input (no spaces). If a skill has no tags, leave it blank.
- Do not include any extra columns.

4) Write output/catalog.json with this structure:
{
  "filters": <exact contents of input/request.json>,
  "counts": {
    "total_in_input": <number of records in input/skills.jsonl>,
    "total_after_category_downloads_date": <count after applying category + min_downloads + min_updated_at filters only>,
    "total_after_keywords": <count after also applying the keyword match>
  },
  "categories": {
    "<each category in request.categories>": [
      {"slug": "...", "name": "...", "downloads": <int>, "stars": <int>, "updated_at": "<ISO8601>", "tags": ["tag1", ...]}
      // exactly the selected top items for this category in the same order as shortlist.csv
    ]
  },
  "generated_at": "<RFC3339 timestamp>"
}
- Include all categories from request.categories as keys under categories, even if a category has an empty array.
- Each item in categories lists must correspond exactly to what you selected for that category in shortlist.csv.

Notes:
- Treat input/skills.jsonl as line-delimited JSON with one skill object per line.
- All keyword checks are case-insensitive substring matches.
- Do not fabricate or alter any skill fields; copy from the input dataset.
- Write the two output files to output/shortlist.csv and output/catalog.json exactly as specified.
